title: TC001-Auth login valid credentials
description: POST /api/v2/auth/login with body {email: 'owner@testsprite.local', password: 'Password123!'} returns 200 and response.data.token is a non-empty string. Response envelope is {success:true, message, data:{user, token, token_type:'Bearer'}}.
code:
import requests

def test_auth_login_valid_credentials():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8000"
    login_url = f"{base_url}/api/v2/auth/login"
    payload = {
        "email": "owner@testsprite.local",
        "password": "Password123!"
    }
    headers = {
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "Accept": "application/json"
    }
    try:
        response = requests.post(login_url, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=30)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request to login endpoint failed: {e}"

    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected status code 200, got {response.status_code}"
    try:
        resp_json = response.json()
    except ValueError:
        assert False, "Response is not a valid JSON"

    # Validate global response envelope
    assert "success" in resp_json, "'success' key missing in response"
    assert resp_json["success"] is True, "Expected success to be True"
    assert "message" in resp_json and isinstance(resp_json["message"], str), "'message' key missing or not string"
    assert "data" in resp_json and isinstance(resp_json["data"], dict), "'data' key missing or not an object"

    # errors key might be missing or null or object
    if "errors" in resp_json:
        assert resp_json["errors"] is None or isinstance(resp_json["errors"], dict), "'errors' key is not null or object"

    data = resp_json["data"]
    # Data should contain user, token and token_type 'Bearer'
    assert "user" in data and isinstance(data["user"], dict), "'user' key missing or not an object in data"
    assert "token" in data and isinstance(data["token"], str), "'token' key missing or not a string in data"
    assert data["token"].strip() != "", "Token string is empty"
    assert "token_type" in data and data["token_type"] == "Bearer", f"token_type is not 'Bearer', got {data.get('token_type')}"

test_auth_login_valid_credentials()

title: TC002-Auth me with bearer token
description: First POST /api/v2/auth/login with owner@testsprite.local/Password123! to get token from response.data.token. Then GET /api/v2/auth/me with header Authorization: Bearer <token>. Expect 200 and data.email == owner@testsprite.local.
code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
API_PREFIX = "/api/v2"
LOGIN_ENDPOINT = f"{BASE_URL}{API_PREFIX}/auth/login"
ME_ENDPOINT = f"{BASE_URL}{API_PREFIX}/auth/me"
TIMEOUT = 30

def test_auth_me_with_bearer_token():
    login_payload = {
        "email": "owner@testsprite.local",
        "password": "Password123!"
    }
    try:
        login_resp = requests.post(
            LOGIN_ENDPOINT,
            json=login_payload,
            timeout=TIMEOUT
        )
        assert login_resp.status_code == 200, f"Login failed with status {login_resp.status_code}"
        login_json = login_resp.json()
        assert login_json.get("success") is True, f"Login response success false: {login_json}"
        data = login_json.get("data")
        assert data is not None, "Login response missing data"
        token = data.get("token")
        token_type = data.get("token_type")
        assert isinstance(token, str) and token, "Token missing or empty"
        assert token_type == "Bearer", f"Unexpected token_type: {token_type}"

        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
        me_resp = requests.get(
            ME_ENDPOINT,
            headers=headers,
            timeout=TIMEOUT
        )
        assert me_resp.status_code == 200, f"Auth me failed with status {me_resp.status_code}"
        me_json = me_resp.json()
        assert me_json.get("success") is True, f"Auth me response success false: {me_json}"
        me_data = me_json.get("data")
        assert me_data is not None, "Auth me response missing data"
        email = me_data.get("email")
        assert email == "owner@testsprite.local", f"Email mismatch: expected owner@testsprite.local but got {email}"
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request failed: {e}"

test_auth_me_with_bearer_token()

title: TC003-Auth me without token returns 401
description: GET /api/v2/auth/me without any Authorization header. Expect HTTP 401.

title: TC004-Catalog list products requires auth
description: GET /api/v2/catalog/products without Authorization. Expect 401. Then login with owner@testsprite.local/Password123! and call again with Bearer token; expect 200 and response.data is a list/array (may be paginated under data.data).
code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8000"
API_PREFIX = "/api/v2"
LOGIN_ENDPOINT = f"{BASE_URL}{API_PREFIX}/auth/login"
CATALOG_PRODUCTS_ENDPOINT = f"{BASE_URL}{API_PREFIX}/catalog/products"
TIMEOUT = 30

def test_catalog_list_products_requires_auth():
    # First try to get catalog products without any Authorization header
    try:
        resp = requests.get(CATALOG_PRODUCTS_ENDPOINT, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request without auth failed unexpectedly: {e}"
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401 Unauthorized without auth, got {resp.status_code}"

    # Login with valid credentials to get Bearer token
    login_payload = {
        "email": "owner@testsprite.local",
        "password": "Password123!"
    }
    headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
    try:
        login_resp = requests.post(LOGIN_ENDPOINT, json=login_payload, headers=headers, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Login request failed unexpectedly: {e}"
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, f"Login failed with status {login_resp.status_code}"
    login_json = login_resp.json()
    assert login_json.get("success") is True, "Login success flag not True"
    data = login_json.get("data")
    assert isinstance(data, dict), "Login response data is not a dict"
    token = data.get("token")
    token_type = data.get("token_type")
    assert token_type == "Bearer", f"Token type is not 'Bearer', got {token_type}"
    assert isinstance(token, str) and token.strip() != "", "Token is empty or missing"

    # Call GET /catalog/products with Authorization header
    auth_headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"
    }
    try:
        catalog_resp = requests.get(CATALOG_PRODUCTS_ENDPOINT, headers=auth_headers, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Authenticated request failed unexpectedly: {e}"

    assert catalog_resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200 OK with auth, got {catalog_resp.status_code}"
    catalog_json = catalog_resp.json()

    assert catalog_json.get("success") is True, "Catalog response success flag not True"
    catalog_data = catalog_json.get("data")
    # Data may be a list or paginated object containing 'data' list
    assert catalog_data is not None, "Catalog response data is None"

    # Determine if data is a list or an object with 'data' key containing list
    if isinstance(catalog_data, dict) and "data" in catalog_data:
        product_list = catalog_data.get("data")
        assert isinstance(product_list, list), "Inner 'data' field is not a list"
    else:
        product_list = catalog_data
        assert isinstance(product_list, list), "Data field is not a list"

test_catalog_list_products_requires_auth()

title: TC005-Plans list public
description: GET /api/v2/plans without auth. Expect 200 and a JSON envelope with data being an array of plan objects.
code:
import requests

def test_plans_list_public():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8000"
    url = f"{base_url}/api/v2/plans"
    headers = {
        "Accept": "application/json",
    }
    try:
        response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
        response.raise_for_status()
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
        assert False, f"Request failed: {e}"

    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status_code}"

    try:
        json_resp = response.json()
    except ValueError:
        assert False, "Response is not a valid JSON"

    # Validate global response envelope keys
    for key in ("success", "message", "data", "errors"):
        assert key in json_resp, f"Missing key '{key}' in response JSON"

    assert isinstance(json_resp["success"], bool), "'success' field should be boolean"
    assert isinstance(json_resp["message"], str), "'message' field should be string"

    # "data" should be an array of plan objects (array != None and is list)
    data = json_resp["data"]
    assert data is not None, "'data' field should not be null"
    assert isinstance(data, list), "'data' field should be a list (array of plan objects)"

    # Optionally check that each item in data is a dict (object)
    for plan in data:
        assert isinstance(plan, dict), "Each plan in data should be an object"

    # errors should be null or an object, but for a successful call should be None
    errors = json_resp.get("errors")
    assert errors is None or isinstance(errors, dict), "'errors' field should be null or an object"

test_plans_list_public()
